_README: SAMPLE routine config for the MorningCoffeeSip cloud ingest routine — a TEMPLATE you adapt, not live config.
It is a plain-English description of the scheduled cloud agent you stand up (see ../../docs/CLOUD-ROUTINE.md).
Your assistant platform's actual routine UI / API will not consume this file verbatim — different platforms
describe a routine differently. This file is the portable, provider-neutral CHECKLIST of every choice a routine
needs, so you can transcribe it into whatever form your platform uses and not forget a field (especially the
read-only connector scope and the deny-list). Copy it, fill in the TODOs, keep every rail.
Nothing here is secret: there are NO tokens, NO account ids, NO credentials — those live in your assistant
account, never in this repo. If you ever find yourself pasting a key in here, stop: that is the irreversible leak.
name: morningcoffeesip-cloud-ingest
description: Scheduled, READ-ONLY ingest routine: reads what is new in email/Drive/calendar, harvests the sent-correspondents list the local message lanes depend on, writes derived sanitized notes (proposals-only) to a review branch. Idle = no-op.

schedule._comment: Hourly is a sane default (fresher brain, more runs). Daily is also fine. Cron is UTC unless your platform says otherwise.

repo._comment: Point the routine at a FRESH CLONE of your MorningCoffeeSip repo. TODO: your fork.
repo._brain_root_note: If your brain root is not the repo's default (./brain), set MCS_BRAIN_ROOT in the routine's env so mcs_paths resolves it the same way the local agent does. See ../../mcs_paths.py and ../../docs/SETUP.md.

connectors._comment: Attach ONLY the connectors you want read, and attach them READ-ONLY. Email is the one that matters most — it produces the correspondents file. Any connector you omit is simply skipped (not an error).

deny_tools: _comment: HARD RAIL. Even with read-only connectors attached, a routine often INHERITS send/mutate tools. Explicitly DENY every one your platform exposes, so the routine cannot send, reply, create/modify a calendar event, move/delete a file, move money, or touch a credential. Fail-closed: if unsure whether a tool can mutate, deny it.

branch._comment: Proposals-only. The routine writes to its OWN branch, never your main. You merge at the /morning gate. A routine on a shared branch CANNOT be technically prevented from pushing by a rule in a file (trust-git-not-self-report) — so the safe posture is a review branch + the automerge gate in github-action-automerge.sample.yml.

standing_prompt_source._comment: The routine's job description = the paste-in prompt in ../../docs/CLOUD-ROUTINE.md (section: 'The paste-in prompt'). That prompt IS the de-welded cloud-refresh skill, inlined. Paste it as the routine's standing instructions; keep ALL of it (read-only · correspondents-from-sent · sanitize-then-derive · proposals-only · write sources/sent-correspondents.txt · idle=no-op).

rails: READ-ONLY on every connector — never send, reply, create/modify a calendar event, move/delete a file, move money, or touch a credential.
No raw secrets ever leave — commit only DERIVED notes; never a raw private body, password, token, or one-time code.
Proposals-only — write to a review branch; never apply to a live brain; never merge to main; never send anything.
Idle = inert — nothing new since the last run means nothing done (no commits, no output).
